text: Postactivation performance enhancement (PAPE) warm-up strategies produce only a very small effect on endurance exercise performance (SMD = 0.15), which is not practically meaningful.
There is very low certainty of evidence that PAPE warm-up routines potentiate endurance performance, and they cannot be recommended for improving endurance exercise outcomes.
Neither the type of PAPE strategy used, the endurance test modality, nor the athlete's VO2max-based endurance level influences the effectiveness of PAPE on endurance performance.
The time interval between a conditioning warm-up routine and the subsequent endurance exercise does not correlate with PAPE effects on endurance performance.
The duration of the endurance exercise does not correlate with PAPE effects, suggesting PAPE is ineffective regardless of whether the endurance event is short or long.
Most studies on PAPE and endurance performance have a high risk of bias due to methodological flaws in randomization and blinding, limiting the reliability of any positive findings.
